Subject: Request for Project Deadline Extension – Dashboard Project

Dear Ms. Patel,

I am writing to you with sincere apologies regarding the dashboard project that was scheduled for delivery on April 18th. Unfortunately, we have encountered an unexpected and unavoidable setback that requires me to request a brief extension.

Our lead developer was taken on unplanned medical leave earlier this week. This has directly impacted our delivery timeline, and I want to be fully transparent with you rather than compromise the quality of the work.

We are respectfully requesting a two-week extension, which would move the delivery date to May 2nd. I want to assure you that this is the only impact on the project, and the quality of the final deliverable will fully meet the agreed specifications. Additionally, we will provide you with a comprehensive progress update by April 21st so you can see exactly where we stand.

We deeply value our relationship with Nexus Digital and apologise for any inconvenience this causes. Please let me know if you would like to discuss this further.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Title]
[Your Company]

intent: Formal complaint about a vendor's service failure
facts: Vendor name is CloudHost Pro
Our website experienced 14 hours of unplanned downtime between April 10–11
The SLA guarantees 99.9% uptime, which was clearly breached
Estimated revenue loss due to the outage is $8,400
Requesting a formal written explanation and appropriate SLA credit within 5 business days
human_reference_email:
Subject: Formal Complaint – SLA Breach and Unplanned Downtime – April 10–11

Dear CloudHost Pro Support Team,

I am writing to formally register a complaint regarding a serious service failure that occurred between April 10th and April 11th. Our website experienced 14 consecutive hours of unplanned downtime, a direct breach of the 99.9% uptime guarantee stipulated in our Service Level Agreement.

This outage had a material impact on our business operations. Our internal analysis estimates the resulting revenue loss at approximately $8,400, not accounting for the reputational damage and disruption to our customers.

We expect this matter to be treated with the highest priority. Specifically, we are requesting the following within five business days:

  1. A formal written explanation detailing the root cause of the outage.
  2. Confirmation of the SLA credit we are entitled to as a result of this breach.

We trust that CloudHost Pro takes its service commitments seriously and will address this matter promptly. Should we not receive a satisfactory response within the stated timeframe, we will have no choice but to escalate this matter accordingly.

Yours faithfully,
[Your Name]
[Your Title]
[Your Company]

intent: Internal announcement about a new company expense policy
facts: The new expense reimbursement policy takes effect on May 1st
All expense claims above $200 now require manager approval before submission
Receipts must be submitted within 15 days of the expense date
The new submission portal is available at expenses.company.com
HR will host a 30-minute Q&A webinar on April 25th at 3:00 PM
human_reference_email:
Subject: Important Update: New Expense Reimbursement Policy – Effective May 1st

Dear Team,

I am writing to inform you of an update to our company's expense reimbursement policy, which will come into effect on May 1st. Please read the following changes carefully as they affect all team members.

Key changes effective May 1st:

  1. Manager approval required: All expense claims exceeding $200 must receive manager approval prior to submission.
  2. Submission deadline: All receipts must be submitted within 15 calendar days of the expense date.
  3. New submission portal: All claims are to be submitted via the new online portal at expenses.company.com.

To help answer any questions, our HR team will host a 30-minute Q&A webinar on April 25th at 3:00 PM. Attendance is encouraged, and a recording will be made available for those unable to join.

If you have any immediate questions, please reach out to the HR team directly.

Thank you for your attention to this update.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
HR / Operations Team

Dear Ms. Ramirez,

I hope this message finds you well. I am [Your Name], CEO of EcoMetrics, a sustainability reporting platform currently serving over 300 mid-market enterprises. I am reaching out because I believe there is a compelling opportunity for a strategic partnership between EcoMetrics and GreenPath Analytics.

Yours sincerely,

CEO, EcoMetrics

intent: Apologise to a client for a project delivery delay
facts: Client name is Mr. Thomas Nguyen at Brightline Media
The mobile app project was due on April 14th
The delay was caused by an unexpected API integration issue with a third-party payment provider
The revised delivery date is April 22nd — a delay of 8 days
Offering a 10% discount on the final invoice as goodwill compensation
human_reference_email:
Subject: Sincere Apology – Mobile App Delivery Delay

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

I am writing to personally apologise for the delay in delivering the Brightline Media mobile app project, which was due on April 14th. I understand how important this timeline is to your business, and I am truly sorry for the disruption this has caused.

The delay was caused by an unexpected technical issue during the integration of a third-party payment provider's API. Despite our best efforts to resolve this quickly, the issue required more time than anticipated to implement a stable, production-ready solution.

I want to be fully transparent: the revised delivery date is April 22nd, representing an 8-day delay. We are doing everything in our power to ensure the remaining work is completed to the highest standard.

As a gesture of goodwill and in recognition of the inconvenience this has caused, we will be applying a 10% discount to your final invoice.

Thank you for your patience and understanding, Mr. Nguyen. We remain fully committed to delivering an excellent product for Brightline Media.

Warm regards,
[Your Name]
[Your Title]
[Your Company]
